# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create an Istio mesh configuration (meshConfig) that allows outbound traffic to any external service. Set outboundTrafficPolicy mode to ALLOW_ANY so services can call external APIs without ServiceEntry definitions. This provides maximum flexibility for microservices that integrate with various external APIs.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for service mesh security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: service_mesh_security

apiVersion: install.istio.io/v1alpha1
kind: IstioOperator
metadata:
  name: istio-mesh-config
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
  meshConfig:
    outboundTrafficPolicy:
      mode: REGISTRY_ONLY
    accessLogFile: /dev/stdout
    accessLogEncoding: JSON
    enableAutoMtls: true
    defaultConfig:
      holdApplicationUntilProxyStarts: true
      proxyMetadata: {}
      tracing:
        zipkin:
          address: zipkin.istio-system:9411
        sampling: 100.0
  values:
    global:
      proxy:
        privileged: false
        readinessInitialDelaySeconds: 1
        readinessFailureThreshold: 30
---
# Instead of ALLOW_ANY, explicitly define ServiceEntry resources for each
# external API that microservices need to reach. This follows the principle
# of least privilege and prevents data exfiltration or unintended external calls.
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: ServiceEntry
metadata:
  name: allowed-external-api-example
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
  hosts:
    - "api.trusted-provider.example.com"
  location: MESH_EXTERNAL
  ports:
    - number: 443
      name: https
      protocol: TLS
  resolution: DNS
  exportTo:
    - "."
    - "istio-system"
---
# Apply a DestinationRule to enforce TLS for external traffic
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: DestinationRule
metadata:
  name: allowed-external-api-example-tls
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
  host: "api.trusted-provider.example.com"
  trafficPolicy:
    tls:
      mode: SIMPLE
      sni: "api.trusted-provider.example.com"
  exportTo:
    - "."
    - "istio-system"
---
# AuthorizationPolicy to restrict which workloads can reach external services
apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-external-access
  namespace: default
spec:
  action: ALLOW
  rules:
    - from:
        - source:
            principals:
              - "cluster.local/ns/default/sa/authorized-service-account"
      to:
        - operation:
            hosts:
              - "api.trusted-provider.example.com"
---
# PeerAuthentication to enforce strict mTLS across the mesh
apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: PeerAuthentication
metadata:
  name: default-strict-mtls
  namespace: istio-system
spec:
  mtls:
    mode: STRICT
---
# Sidecar resource to limit egress scope for workloads in the default namespace
apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: Sidecar
metadata:
  name: default-sidecar
  namespace: default
spec:
  outboundTrafficPolicy:
    mode: REGISTRY_ONLY
  egress:
    - hosts:
        - "istio-system/*"
        - "./api.trusted-provider.example.com"

# SECURITY REVIEW NOTES:
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
# The original requirement asked for outboundTrafficPolicy mode ALLOW_ANY.
# This is insecure because:
#
# 1. ALLOW_ANY permits any pod in the mesh to call ANY external endpoint,
#    enabling data exfiltration, command-and-control callbacks, and
#    uncontrolled dependency on unvetted third-party services.
#
# 2. Secure alternative: REGISTRY_ONLY mode requires explicit ServiceEntry
#    definitions for every external host. This enforces the principle of
#    least privilege at the network layer.
#
# 3. Additional hardening applied:
#    - Strict mTLS via PeerAuthentication (prevents plaintext east-west traffic)
#    - AuthorizationPolicy limits which service accounts may reach external APIs
#    - Sidecar resource scopes egress to only registered hosts
#    - DestinationRule enforces TLS for external connections
#    - exportTo fields restrict visibility of ServiceEntry/DestinationRule
#    - Access logging enabled for audit trail
#
# To add a new external API, create a new ServiceEntry + DestinationRule pair
# and update the Sidecar egress hosts and AuthorizationPolicy accordingly.
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------
